blockers: Missing mandatory AEGIS stages in requirements-lock downstream validation: L3_CODE_REVIEW and L3_SECURITY_AUDIT are omitted from downstream_validation in requirements-lock.json, creating a traceability gap for locked-requirement hash validation.

Frontend-only tasks for features requiring backend API work: T13 (threaded replies), T14 (emoji reactions), and T15 (message search) are assigned to frontend-squad without explicit backend API tasks covering reactions, search, or thread-specific endpoints.

Accessibility validation scheduled too late: T19 depends on T18, placing WCAG 2.1 AA fixes at the very end of the schedule. An earlier accessibility checkpoint should be added after core components are built.
suggestions: Clarify the AI trigger mechanism (e.g., @assistant mention or automatic routing rules) in the PRD or architecture.
Add explicit client-side reconnection logic for SSE and WebSocket failures to the boundary-conditions section.
Document a dependency-version pinning policy (e.g., package-lock.json, exact Docker image tags) for reproducible demo builds.
Add a dedicated i18n setup task to the task breakdown to support the PRD's Simplified Chinese and English default languages.
Fix the deployment service count in architecture.md Section 8 (currently says '3 services' but lists 4).
